text: This video demonstrates a modification to the standard TA-TME technique consisting of reinforcing the purse string. The Norwegian group has recently reported a high rate of local recurrence after TA-TME for colorectal cancer that shows an unusually early and multifocal pattern. Causes for this unexpected issue are unclear but could be related not with specimen quality but with technical aspects, since this recurrence pattern has not been previously observed with TATA procedures. A possible explanation could be the inadequate tightness of the purse string and the transanal insufflation required to complete the pelvic dissection.

We propose to add extra steps to the standard TA-TME technique in order to prevent this from occurring. Reinforcing the purse string ensures a complete occlusion of the rectal lumen and an airtight seal preventing the theoretical risk of tumor cell spillage and dissemination into the pelvic cavity. So, after full thickness rectal transection, we suggest reinforcing the purse string with a double continuous suture and also repeating the pelvic washout before continuing with further dissection. The following video details these extra steps.

The surgeon ties the purse string and performs the first wall shot of the rectal stump, followed by a circumferential full thickness rectotomy to free the rectal wall. A double continuous suture with a V-lock suture is then applied to reinforce the purse string, ensuring complete inversion of the rectal mucosa and an airtight seal.
The surgeon performs a second generous washout of the pelvic cavity before continuing with the down to up dissection through the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane.
These apparently simple extra steps may help reduce local recurrence rates, particularly the multifocal pattern. However, long-term oncological results are still awaited.
